dataset: USLCI (U.S. Life Cycle Inventory Database) and TRACI 2.1 LCIA Methods

pi_items.source: Mathematical calculation: 20 metric tons × 400 km = 8,000 ton-kilometers. Verified via Python computation.
Mathematical calculation: 20 metric tons × 80 km = 1,600 ton-kilometers. Verified via Python computation.

pi_items.verified_value: Real, publicly available U.S. Life Cycle Inventory Database maintained by NREL, downloaded from GitHub: https://github.com/FLCAC-admin/uslci-content
pi_items.source: GitHub repository FLCAC-admin/uslci-content confirmed real and maintained by NREL/FLCAC. Contains 550+ LCI processes for U.S. production data.

pi_items.verified_value: Real TRACI v2.1 LCIA methods from EPA, available via Figshare article 25782786 (Ag Data Commons)
pi_items.source: Figshare article 25782786 titled 'TRACIv2.1 for FEDEFLv1.2' confirmed real, authored by Ben Young, hosted on USDA Ag Data Commons

pi_items.verified_value: Global Warming (part of TRACI 2.1 impact categories)
pi_items.source: EPA documentation confirms TRACI 2.1 includes 'Global Warming' as one of ten impact categories. GWP (Global Warming Potential) quantifies CO2-equivalent emissions.
Specification in task description. Feasible for steel shipment via rail-truck intermodal route.

Reasonable for Chicago-area to Wisconsin intermodal route. Chicago-Wisconsin corridor documented as active intermodal shipping lane with typical distances 320-640 km depending on specific origin/destination.
Reasonable for local drayage (last-mile trucking) component of intermodal route from Wisconsin rail terminal to final destination.

pi_items.metadata_value: Intermodal freight route between Chicago and Wisconsin
pi_items.verified_value: Chicago-Wisconsin intermodal corridor is documented as active shipping lane with rail-truck combination shipments common due to logistics hub role of Chicago area
pi_items.source: Wisconsin DOT Freight Advisory Committee documentation and multiple freight service provider confirmations of Chicago-Wisconsin intermodal operations

pi_items.metadata_value: USLCI contains 'Transport, train, diesel powered' and 'Transport, combination truck, diesel powered' processes
pi_items.verified_value: USLCI confirmed to contain updated rail transport processes (11 Class I rail datasets) and truck transport processes (324+ EPA MOVES datasets) in openLCA JSON-LD format
pi_items.source: USLCI Quarterly Release documentation confirms comprehensive transport data from NREL/ERG, compatible with openLCA 2.x

privileged_info_summary: The task uses two verified real datasets: (1) the USLCI (U.S. Life Cycle Inventory Database) from NREL containing 550+ processes including rail and truck transport, downloadable from GitHub FLCAC-admin/uslci-content, and (2) TRACI 2.1 LCIA methods published by EPA/USDA, available via Figshare article 25782786. The shipment calculations are mathematically correct: 20 metric tons × 400 km rail = 8,000 ton-kilometers; 20 metric tons × 80 km truck = 1,600 ton-kilometers. The Chicago-Wisconsin intermodal route described is realistic for the specified distances. The Global Warming impact category is confirmed as part of TRACI 2.1. All data sources are public and non-synthetic.
